mission.title: Install and Configure kubeadm (Kubernetes bootstrapper)
mission.description: Kubeadm is a tool designed to simplify the process of creating and managing Kubernetes clusters. It provides a straightforward way to bootstrap a Kubernetes control plane and join worker nodes, making it ideal for users who prefer a hands-on approach to cluster management. However, it requires a good understanding of Kubernetes concepts and may not be as user-friendly as managed solutions.

steps.title: Install Kubeadm
steps.description:
This step installs the kubeadm binary on your system, which is necessary for bootstrapping the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -s https://packages.cloud.google.com/apt/doc/apt-key.gpg | sudo apt-key add -

echo 'deb https://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list

sudo apt-get update

sudo apt-get install -y kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00

```

> Add Google Cloud public key for package verification.
> Add Kubernetes APT repository.
> Update the package list.
> Install specific versions of kubelet, kubeadm, and kubectl.
commands: echo 'deb https://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list
sudo apt-get install -y kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00

steps.title: Initialize the Kubernetes Control Plane
steps.description:
This command bootstraps the control plane for your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

sudo kubeadm init --kubernetes-version=v1.31.0 --pod-network-cidr=192.168.0.0/16

```

> Initialize the cluster with a specific Kubernetes version and pod network CIDR.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig for the Regular User
steps.description:
This step configures kubeconfig for the non-root user to manage the cluster.

```bash

mkdir -p $HOME/.kube

sudo cp -i /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf $HOME/.kube/config

sudo chown $(id -u):$(id -g) $HOME/.kube/config

```

> Create the .kube directory.
> Copy the admin kubeconfig to the user's home directory.
> Change ownership of the kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Install a Pod Network Add-on
steps.description:
This step installs a network add-on to enable communication between pods.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://docs.projectcalico.org/manifests/calico.yaml

```

> Install Calico as the network add-on.

steps.title: Verify the Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in the cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes and their statuses.

uninstall.title: Reset the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command will revert any changes made by kubeadm init or kubeadm join. Warning: This will remove all cluster data.

```bash

sudo kubeadm reset

sudo rm -rf /etc/cni/net.d

```

> Reset the cluster.
> Remove CNI configurations.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Backup the current configuration, drain the node, upgrade the cluster, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

sudo kubeadm upgrade plan

sudo kubeadm upgrade apply v1.31.0

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check the current node status before upgrading.
> Drain the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Check available upgrades.
> Upgrade the cluster to the specified version.
> Uncordon the node after upgrade.
> Verify the nodes are ready after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubelet.service: Failed to start kubelet.service: Unit kubelet.service not found.'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubelet is not installed or not properly configured.

**Fix:**
Reinstall kubelet using 'sudo apt-get install -y kubelet=1.31.0-00'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:8080: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubelet is not running or kubeconfig is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check kubelet status with 'systemctl status kubelet' and ensure kubeconfig is correctly set.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeadm init failed: error while running 'kubelet': exit status 1'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubelet may not be installed or there are conflicting configurations.

**Fix:**
Ensure kubelet is installed and check for conflicting configurations in /etc/kubernetes.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'pod network not ready: cni config not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The network add-on has not been installed.

**Fix:**
Install a network add-on using 'kubectl apply -f <network-addon-yaml>'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new features like PodSecurity admission controller and improved kubelet configuration options.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the --pod-network-cidr flag in favor of more flexible network configurations.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure to review the new network configuration options.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' status.
codeSnippets: echo 'deb https://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list
sudo apt-get install -y kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00

prerequisites.description: Ensure your system meets the minimum requirements for running Kubernetes.
